page: Vultr vcg-a100-96c-896g-320vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server configured with 96 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing AMD processors. The system features 896.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 9.33 GB of RAM per core to support memory-intensive operations.

This server includes dedicated hardware acceleration consisting of four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs, delivering a total of 320 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 3072 GB NVMe SSD, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Given the dense GPU and memory configuration, this server is designed for heavy computational workloads such as large language model training, deep learning, and complex data science jobs. The high resource density of AMD vCPUs, massive system memory, and NVMe storage provides a specialized, high-capacity hardware profile for enterprise-scale parallel processing tasks.
description: Vultr vcg-a100-96c-896g-320vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server designed for high-performance parallel computing. Built on the x86_64 architecture with AMD processors, it features 96 vCPUs and 896.0 GB of system memory, yielding 9.33 GB of RAM per core. The server is equipped with four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs that provide a total of 320 GB of VRAM, making it highly capable for intensive graphical and mathematical computations. Storage is supported by a 3072 GB NVMe SSD, and the system includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. This hardware profile is optimized for demanding enterprise workloads, including large-scale machine learning, deep learning model training, and complex data science simulations that require substantial GPU memory and high-speed local storage.
